LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE OCT-12-2022 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS NICHOLAS PROIETTI AND NICHOLAS VINCENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS-COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. UNOPPOSED MOTION GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)
DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT BEAUVAIS, GERRI HEARING SET FOR DEC-20-2022 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, CROSS DEFENDANT GERRI BEAUVAIS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41, WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE PARTIES CONDUCT A "MEET AND CONFER IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE" BEFORE A DEMURRER IS FILED. A DECLARATION MUST THEN BE FILED WHICH SPECIFIES WHETHER THE MEET AND CONFER WAS IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY AND THE RESPONSE TO THE NOW DUE BY JANUARY 20, 2023. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/RBU)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF GERRI BEAUVAIS'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE (1) AND MONETARY SANCTION. CONTINUED TO JANUARY 3, 2023 AT 9AM IN DEPARTMENT 301. THE COURT FINDS THAT INSUFFICIENT MEET AND CONFER EFFORTS HAVE OCCURRED TO OBVIATE THE NEED FOR THIS MOTION. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER BY DECEMBER 28, 2023 AND REPORT BACK TO THE COURT VIA EMAIL TO JPT MCDONALD BY DECEMBER 30, 2022. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO REVIEW THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT'S DECISION IN COITO V. SUPERIOR COURT, 54 CAL. 4TH 480 (2012) WHERE THE PERIMETERS OF RESPONSES TO THESE FORM INTERROGATORIES ARE EXTENSIVELY DISCUSSED. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

No Appearances.

Defendant NICHOLAS PROIETTI's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant NICHOLAS PROIETTI's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the complaint's second cause of action for breach of contract is granted with leave to amend. Such a motion may be filed at any time before final judgment. (See, e.g., Sofias v. Bank of America (1985) 172 Cal.App.3d 583, 585.) Discovery responses do not plead a cause of action. No sanctions are awarded.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Notice Of Motion And Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Gerri Beauvais In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One (1) And Monetary Sanction
Off Calendar Mar-16-2023 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2023 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS DEFENDANT GERRI BEAUVAIS' DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT, as follows:

Cross-defendant Gerri Beauvais's (Beauvais) demurrer is overruled.

"A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable." (County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 996, 1008-1009.) "No other extrinsic evidence can be considered." (Edmon & Karnow, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 7:8 (Rutter: 2022).)

Beauvais demurrers to the first amended cross-complaint on the grounds that cross-complainant Nicholas Proietti, an individual, dba Nicholas Vincent Design (Proietti) lacks standing and capacity to sue.

First, Beauvais argues that Proietti's alleged false statements on his Fictitious Business Name Statement renders him without standing to sue in his cross-complaint. Not so. Beauvais filed a complaint against Proietti. A cross-complaint may be filed by the original defendant. (CCP sec. 428.10.)

Second, Beauvais argues that Proietti lacks capacity to sue, mainly relying on discovery responses. (See P&A at subsection 2(a), 2(c), and 2(d).) Discovery responses cannot be considered in a demurrer. (See Rutter 7:8.)

Third, Beauvais argues that Proietti's failure to allege that he was a duly licensed contractor renders Proietti without capacity to sue, citing the Contractors State Licensing Law (Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 7031.) This law requires an entity "engaged in the business or acted in the capacity of a contractor" seeking recovery of a contract to have a contractor's license. (Id.) But Beauvais does not identify which portion(s) of the first amended cross-complaint subject Proietti to the Contractors State Licensing Law. To the extent that this argument also relies on discovery responses, it must similarly be excluded from consideration.

Finally, Beauvais argues that Proietti lacks capacity to sue because "Since Cross-complainant would be required to state that he has committed crimes (misdemeanors) in order to proceed with his Cross-complaint, the Contract in question is voided." (Opp. at 5:03-04.) This argument invites the court to reach factual determinations not appropriate for a demurrer. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; see also Rutter 7:8.)

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS DEFENDANT GERRI BEAUVAIS' DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

Gerri Beauvais' demurrer is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41, which requires that the parties conduct a "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before a demurrer is filed. A declaration must then be filed which specifies whether the meet and confer was in person or by telephone. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and the response to the now due by January 20, 2023.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
